channel.title: EXHIBITION! on Diptanuj Sarkar
channel.description: Recent content in EXHIBITION! on Diptanuj Sarkar

item.description: This document should essentially spell out everything related to the exhibition - best if there are two versions. One will be short and ideal for advertisement purposes (TALK TO MEDIA DESIGN), and the other more detailed (intended for mailing to school contacts). Topics that should be touched - A brief introduction of IISER-K, The exhibition, its aims and organisation. Schedule of the exhibition Themes and Sub-Themes for the exhibition Salient features of the exhibition (highlight prestige, prizes, etc) Guidelines for participation (Point-wise list of rules that are deemed to be most important.

Outreach themed FAQs What is an IISER? etc, iiseradmission type Registration Procedure themed FAQs Where and when can a school register for the science exhibition? What do we do if we missed the registration process? When does the registration process begin? When does the registration process end? What is the eligibility criteria for participating teams? What are the categories for the exhibition? What will happen if the school registered but unable to participate due to some issue?

The login and submission portal is crucial in this case. The screening requires that a foolproof (and secure) means of uploading submissions exist. This needs to be decided on in conversation with Web Dev team Important features System for storing and indexing candidate contact info. Allowing candidates to upload Documents (Write Up, Verification, etc) Allowing candidates to upload videos (Youtube links? Discuss)

Just an outline for now - might need to look some examples, and definitely need to discuss this with the group. The recommendation must be done by the mentor teacher who has been registered with the team. We will send the forms to the teachers and get the recommendations from them confidentially - all NIUS Style Enquire about previous Exhibition experience of participants Ask them to grade the academic / scientific capabilities of the students in the team.

Long ways away, many details need to be fleshed out, such as - Arena requirements Food for students Seating arrangements for teachers and parents Judge Panel Judgement criteria Booking and logistics of award ceremony Prizes

Selection will be done by volunteers from Exhibition working group. Most of the selection work will essentially involve weeding out low quality projects. Mostly easy to do, a few hours a week ought to do it. When we boil it down to the last few good projects, we can ask Profs for their opinions for final selections. (Reason: By that point we will hopefully have the judge panel finalised). Important factors - Create a transparent method of selection.

Factors that must be considered for determination of fee amount The costs that will be expended per team of 3 from our side (reqs will be fleshed out in detail when work on what-we-will-provide to participants is finalised) (Reason: Not knowing much finance know-how, I will only seek to tell finance what is required and how much we can ask for as fees max) Feasibility of fees - 1.5k to 2k is considered reasonable for such exhibitions.

How the process should go for the participants -> STEP 1: 1.1 Register 1.2 [Payment of Fees](https://casiowave.github.io/exhibition/payment-of-fees/) 1.3 [Get credentials - login system (?)](https://casiowave.github.io/exhibition/get-credentials-login-system-/) STEP 2: Project Dev and Submission 2.1 Project Ideation - Identify theme, explore topics, develop hypothesis, model in discussion with Mentor Teacher 2.2 [Development of Project - Posters, Model, Presentation, surveys, evidence, etc](https://casiowave.github.io/exhibition/development-of-project-posters-model-presentation-surveys-evidence-etc/) 2.3 [Project Submission - Upload Project details, themes, description, URL to a video demonstrating the project, and evidence.

There will be an overarching theme of the exhibition - with fixed sub-themes. Theme should be something that interests schoolchildren and/or is the generic I-wanna-save-the-world type. Any new ideas appreciated - THIS IS SUBJECT TO FURTHER DISCUSSION IN EXHIBITION GROUP. We will provide content to teams that outline what kind of exhibitions we are looking for - with no ambiguity, but keeping in mind that we need this to be as flexible as humanly possible.

Important points to keep in mind regarding this - Guidance needs to be provided at this step - people screw up in strange places Again, discussion with Web Dev to chalk out how this is actually going to happen.

Registration of the participants must be done early. Schedule (Approx) Registration stays open for about 2 months (Reason: Sometimes teams have to come up the project before they actually register - this will help) - OPEN IN NOVEMBER Screening will take about 1 month (Reason: Expect a substantial amount of registrations - many of them will not be of meaningful quality. Must be weeded down to 50-60 (Abhra’s estimate of max teams of 3)) - JANUARY Selection of teams must be declared about 2-3 weeks in advance of exhibition dates (Reason: Good projects often have a lot of last moment ideas + If there are out-of-station teams, they need time to plan commute and stay) Exhibition will preferably be over 2-3 days minimum, last day for award ceremony (Reason: Increases attendance to fest, allows judges to review in detail without a rush) Note: RN Tagore needs to be booked for award ceremony on last day.

Basic requirements Table - per team Must be stable - exhibits CAN NOT be put at risk 3 chairs (max) - per team Optional 4th for teacher - I do not prefer if as it is best if the students are unaided in the exhibition itself. But depends on what seating arrangements have been made. Tablecloth - per team Essential for proper display - might seem decorative, but quite necessary.
Needs to be decided on in discussion with Exhibition Working Group ASAP. Mail profs and ask for consent. A plan should be prepped beforehand - so that Profs don’t find the entire operation shabby.
